Governance-centric agentic software engineering

Agent Governance Mechanisms

Generative AI is shifting software engineering from a practice built around scarce implementation toward one built around abundant, low-cost code. The hard part stops being writing code and becomes governing the conditions under which fast code can be trusted — keeping it inspectable, correctable, and maintainable at speed.

Between two schools of thought

Two common ways to build with agents sit at opposite poles. This site is about the midway.

Vibe coding

Prompt an agent, accept what looks right, iterate by feel. Fast and fluid — but quality rests on the model and your eye. At scale the same failures keep recurring, and human review becomes the bottleneck.

Examples:
  • Karpathy — coined “vibe coding”
  • Steve Yegge’s Gas Town
all velocity — no durable guardrails

Governance-centric

The midway. Velocity exposes failures; you convert each recurring one into a guardrail — a type, a lint, a gate. The guardrails grow out of real failures, so code stays fast and stays trustworthy.

velocity + guardrails grown from failure

Spec-driven development

Write a precise specification up front, then generate and verify against it. Rigorous — but it front-loads all the judgment: you must know every constraint before the agent acts, and specs rarely anticipate what only breaks at velocity.

Examples:
  • Meyer, CACM — “From Probable to Provable”
  • vibe-OS / vibe-tools (formal tooling for AI)
all guardrails — specified up front

← all velocity   •   all guardrails →

The midway has a name — governance conversion — and a working rule: don't specify everything up front, and don't trust the vibes; let velocity surface the failures, and convert each recurring one into a durable guardrail. The result is 51 governance mechanisms across three roles, each written like a design pattern — the recurring failure it kills, and why it is not just the cheaper thing everyone already does.

The way of thinking

Three stances that make the midway work — distilled from the AI-First Engineering Method (21 principles); the full set ships in the starter CLAUDE.md.

Architect deliberately

  • Implementation is cheap; architecture compounds — buy the right design, not the fast one.
  • Name shapes with types; primitive-passing leaves the architecture anonymous.
  • Make models, state, and policy explicit — state machines over scattered counters, enums over magic strings.
  • One canonical way beats many clever ones — agents will apply patterns they see 200× with no debate.
  • Attack accidental complexity; budget for the essential kind.

Convert failure into machinery

  • When a failure recurs, encode it — a lint, type, gate, or schema — instead of re-inspecting for it.
  • Move audits to lints: cheap, at-commit, deterministic beats expensive and post-hoc.
  • Let the compiler and gates hold the line — review is not a substitute for static analysis.
  • Never fail quiet — every caught error logs, re-throws, or is justified in a comment.
  • No compatibility shims — migrate every call site in the same change.

Keep judgment scarce & central

  • Carry work autonomously; surface only the load-bearing, architectural calls.
  • Hyper-experimentation — pilot, compare, measure; a cheap experiment beats a debate, and negative results are wins.
  • Verify claims and trust nothing stale — re-run the gates yourself, because markers rot.
  • Reason about second-order dynamics — what happens at T+100, or under concurrency?
  • Documentation encodes invariants that drive tests, not prose that rots.
Case study: Cheap Code, Costly Judgment: A Case Study on Governable Agentic Software Engineering
Live system it governs: scholaccess.com

Walking through the loop

Governance conversion is a non-terminating loop: higher velocity keeps surfacing failure classes that earlier governance didn't address.

1Velocity exposes failureagent changes surface ambiguity, drift, weak boundaries — fast
→
2A human classifies itlocal defect, or a recurring structural weakness?
→
3Convert to governanceencode it: a type, lint, schema, gate, or harness rule
→
4Action space narrowsevery later agent inherits a smaller, more explicit space
→
5Governability compoundsthe environment absorbs more agent work; velocity is sustainable

Implementation is cheap; the judgment that decides which governance should exist is the costly, human part. Two dual theses hold it together: governance makes velocity sustainable, and judgment determines which governance should exist. The catalogue below is the repertoire this loop produced in one real production system.

The control-map figurethe whole catalogue at a glance — four views, controls clickable Interactive viewsevery control, re-grouped live from metadata Quick startadopt these in your own repo Browse on GitHubREADME · INDEX · every control's full writeup Starter CLAUDE.mda real, mature one — redacted; a menu to adapt Download the catalogueall writeups as a markdown ZIP

The catalogue — every control

Agent

Context & dispatch substrate

What an agent knows and how it is launched.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Brief-linting ★validationnovelHard
Docs hierarchy + governance indexvalidationstandardSoft·Hard
Dynamic context injectionagent-outputnovelSoft
Role-typed dispatchquality-gatenotableHard

Gates & merge-train

The path-to-production staircase for agent work.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Pre-commit hook (3-stanza, tree-sha markers) ★quality-gatenotableHard
Sentinel first-commit early-abortquality-gatenovelHard
Merge-train MIS batchingquality-gatenotableHard
Staged deploy gates (canary → smoke → promote)quality-gatestandardHard

Mediators & resource locks

Host-level wrappers that ration shared compute across concurrent worktrees.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Test-serializer (N=1 flock on dotnet test) ★regressionnotableHard
Build-serializer (M=8 semaphore)validationnotableHard
Aggregate-compute protection (lint-all host mutex)validationstandardHard

Lifecycle & observability

Live signal surfaces over the fleet.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Agent-registry (agent-registry.jsonl + marker cache) ★observabilitynotableHard (signal)
Typed event bus + playbookobservabilitynotableHard (signal)
Deploy heartbeats + stale-worker detectionobservabilitystandardHard (signal)
Tombstone commits (lifecycle close records)audit-trailnotableHard (audit)
Cron-alerts gateobservabilitynotableHard (blocking)

Governance-doc controls

Documentation treated as enforced infrastructure.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
CLAUDE.md rule index + cap lint ★validationnotableSoft·Hard
Mandatory snippet-table enforcementvalidationnotableHard
Epic Definition-of-Done (Final-Opus trust-nothing re-run)quality-gatenotableHard
Doc-hygiene lints (index coverage, autogen provenance)validationstandardHard

Models-bridge

System models

The typed models the fleet reasons through and the codebase is generated from — the MBSE bridge.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Executable source-of-truth (data-not-code, can't drift) ★typed-irnovelHard
Component & zone modeltyped-irnotableHard
Synchronization model (meta-sync)typed-irnotableHard
Mediator & single-writer contractstyped-irnotableHard
Service-flow / API modeltyped-irnotableHard
Deployment & tier topologytyped-irnotableHard
Domain registriestyped-irstandardHard
Drift & parity gates (model↔reality)validationnotableHard
Model-driven codegenvalidationnotableHard
Model query surface (repo-query)agent-outputnotableSoft
Meta-model consumption (read, don't hardcode)typed-irnotableHard

Product

Canonical models & seams

The one sanctioned typed model or seam per concern, each held in place by a ban-lint.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
PdfModel (sole PDF mutation surface) ★typed-irnotableHard
Office Models ({Slides,Docs,Sheets}Model)typed-irnotableHard
ServiceClient (typed cross-service seam)bounded-servicenotableHard
Canonical walkers (one traversal per tree)typed-irstandardHard
Sole raw-Redis seam (the dispatch module)bounded-servicestandardHard

Validation & conformance

Deterministic pass/fail checks over the artifact.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
ContentValidator (input ⊆ output fidelity) ★validationnotableHard
Blocking semantic lintsvalidationstandardHard
Standards / WCAG rule enginevalidationnotableHard
Cross-source coherence lintsvalidationnotableHard

Regression tests

Repeatable behaviour-pinning bodies.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Test-onion tiers (Smoke / Lite / targeted / full) ★regressionnotableHard
FsCheck property testsregressionnotableHard
Fuzz campaigns (+ auto-coverage)regressionnotableHard
DDT pin-trailersregressionnotableHard

Provenance & attribution

Durable records of what the tool changed.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Per-mutator attribution stamps ★audit-trailnotableHard (audit)
F10 mutator-stamp-wiring lintvalidationnotableHard
derive-changelog (reconstruct mutations)audit-trailnotableHard (audit)
a11y_ prefix conventionrepair-vocabstandardHard

Repair vocabulary

The bounded move-space of the remediator.

ControlFormNoveltyEnf.
Typed ViolationCategory / FailureCategory enums ★repair-vocabnotableHard
Closed remediation-verb setsrepair-vocabnotableHard
Codemod-first threshold (N≳50 → AST transformer)repair-vocabnotableSoft

Built from the markdown by catalog.py build. Hover a control for its one-line summary; click to open its writeup.
